page_content:
ASTR480 High Energy Astrophysics (3 Credits)

The structure, formation, and astrophysics of compact objects, such as white dwarfs, neutron stars, and black holes, are examined. Phenomena such as supernovae and high-energy particles are also covered. Prerequisite: Must have completed or be concurrently enrolled in ASTR320; or permission of CMNS-Astronomy department.

ASTR498 Special Problems in Astronomy (1-6 Credits) Research or special study. Credit according to work done. Restriction: Must be in one of the following programs (Physics; Astronomy) ; and permission of CMNS-Astronomy department.

2025-2026 [updated 2025.04.17]
